A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

* A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

SUITE 120
BOO NORTH HAVEN
POST OFFICE BOX 4360
ONTARIO, CALIFORNIA 91761
TELEPHONE (714) 985-8584
TELECOPIER (714) 944-1441

OF COUNSEL
JAMES B. CORISON

OFFICES IN
RIVERSIDE (714)686-1450
PALM SPRINGS (619)325-7264
RANCHO MIRAGE (619)568-2611

November 5, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95804-0814

Re:  Informal  Advice  Regarding  City  of  Fontana
     Councilmember David Eshleman

Dear Sir/Madam:

     We are the City Attorney for the City of Fontana, and are
requesting  informal advice on behalf of Councilmember David
Eshleman.  In particular, Councilmember Eshleman requests advice
concerning whether his ownership of 14 shares of Fontana Union
Water Company precludes him from participating in decision making
as a Councilmember of the City of Fontana concerning the potential
provision of water services by the City, and concerning related
litigation.  Both of these decisions are potentially adverse to the
Fontana Water Company (not the Fontana Union Water Company in which
Councilmember Eshleman owns shares).

     Fontana Union Water Company (the company in which
Councilmember Eshleman holds shares) is currently,
its wells are being operated and its customers are being serviced
by the Fontana Water Company.  The Fontana Water Company is a
separate entity in which Councilmember Eshleman has no interest.

     We are enclosing the Fontana Union Water Company's
Articles of Incorporation, its bylaws and its IRS designation as a
non-profit tax-exempt entity.  This material shows that Fontana
Union Water Company is a non-profit mutual water company.

RJN21095

LAW OFFICES OF

BEST, BEST & KRIEGER


Fair Political Practices Commission
November 5, 1992
Page 2


Councilmember Eshleman informs us that the 14 shares entitle him to
receive a certain amount of irrigation water (a percentage of the
state yield), and that the shares do not currently pay him any
dividends. Likewise, he informs us that the Fontana Union Water
Company has not been the source of any gifts to Councilmember
Eshleman. In addition, both Councilmember Eshleman and Tom
McPeters, Esq. (the attorney for Fontana Union Water Company) have
informed us that the shares can be sold separately from land (they
are not appurtenant).

In the future, the City will evaluate water decisions and
service issues involving or which may affect the Fontana Water
Company. In the future, the City may also need to make decisions
involving litigation that potentially is adverse to Fontana Water
Company. Therefore, Councilmember Eshleman needs to know if the
conflict of interest laws set forth in the Political Reform Act
permit him to participate in these City decisions. Councilmember
Eshleman also needs to know whether those conflict of interest laws
would allow him to participate in other City decisions that
directly involve or materially indirectly affect the Fontana Union
Water Company.

In researching this matter, our office has become aware
of an informal advice letter which the Fair Political Practices
Commission (FPPC) has issued to a requestor on behalf of Margaret
C. Wright (copy attached). It appears that based upon the FPPC's
analysis set forth in the Wright letter, Councilmember Eshleman
would not be precluded from participating in the above described
decision making due to his ownership of shares of the Fontana Union
Water Company. Like the shares in Wright, Councilmember Eshleman's
shares do not fit within any of the categories of a "financial
interest" contained in Government Code Section 87103. This is
because the shares do not constitute a "source of income (or
gifts)," and they do not constitute an interest in a "business
entity" or "real property" under the analysis in the Wright
opinion. However, the Wright informal advice letter was issued in
1985 and Councilmember Eshleman would like to verify that the
FPPC's current opinion on the classification of shares in non-
profit mutual water companies remains the same as in 1985.




RJN21095

LAW OFFICES OF

BEST, BEST & KRIEGER

Fair Political Practices Commission
November 5, 1992
Page 3


     Please feel free to contact us if you need any further
information to address this inquiry.  Thank you very much for your
assistance in this matter.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]

                                        Stephen P. Deitsch
                                        of BEST, BEST & KRIEGER


Enclosures

cc:  David Eshleman, Councilmember
     Jay Corey, City Manager
     Clark H. Alsop, City Attorney




RJN21095

embedding.qa_text: November 5, 1992 Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 Sacramento, CA 95804-0814 Re: Informal Advice Regarding City of Fontana Councilmember David Eshleman Dear Sir/Madam: We are the City Attorney for the City of Fontana, and are requesting informal advice on behalf of Councilmember David Eshleman. In particular, Councilmember Eshleman requests advice concerning whether his ownership of 14 shares of Fontana Union Water Company precludes him from participating in decision making as a Councilmember of the City of Fontana concerning the potential provision of water services by the City, and concerning related litigation. Both of these decisions are potentially adverse to the Fontana Water Company (not the Fontana Union Water Company in which Councilmember Eshleman owns shares). Fontana Union Water Company (the company in which Councilmember Eshleman holds shares) is in bankruptcy. Currently, its wells are being operated and its customers are being serviced by the Fontana Water Company. The Fontana Water Company is a separate entity in which Councilmember Eshleman has no interest. We are enclosing the Fontana Union Water Company's Articles of Incorporation, its bylaws and its IRS designation as a non-profit tax-exempt entity. This material shows that Fontana Union Water Company is a non-profit mutual water company. Councilmember Eshleman informs us that the 14 shares entitle him to receive a certain amount of irrigation water (a percentage of the safe yield), and that the shares do not currently pay him any dividends. Likewise, he informs us that the Fontana Union Water Company has not been the source of any gifts to Councilmember Eshleman. In addition, both Councilmember Eshleman and Tom McPeters, Esq. (the attorney for Fontana Union Water Company) have informed us that the shares can be sold separately from land (they are not appurtenant). In the future, the City will evaluate water decisions and service issues involving or which may affect the Fontana Water Company. In the future, the City may also need to make decisions involving litigation that potentially is adverse to Fontana Water Company. Therefore, Councilmember Eshleman needs to know if the conflict of interest laws set forth in the Political Reform Act permit him to participate in these City decisions. Councilmember Eshleman also needs to know whether those conflict of interest laws would allow him to participate in other City decisions that directly involve or materially indirectly affect the Fontana Union Water Company. In researching this matter, our office has become aware of an informal advice letter which the Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC) has issued to a requestor on behalf of Margaret C. Wright (copy attached). It appears that based upon the FPPC's analysis set forth in the Wright letter, Councilmember Eshleman would not be precluded from participating in the above described decision making due to his ownership of shares of the Fontana Union Water Company. Like the shares in Wright, Councilmember Eshleman's shares do not fit within any of the categories of a "financial interest" contained in Government Code Section 87103.
